name: Randomized TPE improved multi-omic biological-age markers
summary: A randomized placebo-controlled human trial reported favorable multi-omic aging-marker changes after TPE-based intervention, strongest with TPE plus IVIG.
statement: In 42 adults over 50, TPE-based treatment arms showed favorable changes across epigenetic, proteomic, glycomic, metabolomic, immune, and biological-age measures compared with sham, with the strongest signal reported for TPE plus IVIG.
population_or_model: Adults older than 50 years in a randomized placebo-controlled trial
time_horizon: Intervention period with multi-omic post-treatment follow-up
quantitative_note: This is the strongest staged human aging-biomarker support, but it remains a surrogate-endpoint trial.
caveats: The study measured biomarkers rather than intrinsic-capacity, disease, or mortality endpoints.
The sample size was small for multi-omic subgroup inference.
TPE plus IVIG is an invasive combination and cannot be generalized to simple young-plasma or single-factor claims.
